name: dmatrix_server
_comment: d-Matrix single-server reference (8 cards × 8 chiplets = 64 chiplets), modeled at chiplet granularity. Identical per-chiplet device spec as `dmatrix.squadrack`. peak_flops_TF=150 stores the per-chiplet **FP16-equivalent** dense peak (= MXINT8/2 = 300/2; framework convention is FP16 dense per chip with linear byte-ratio scaling for lower precisions). Linear scaling gives MXINT8 = 300 TF/chiplet (= 2400 TF/card, matches whitepaper) and MXINT4 = 600 TF/chiplet by simple byte ratio. **Caveat**: published d-Matrix MXINT4 is 1200 TF/chiplet (4× MXINT8, not 2×) due to block-sparse acceleration in the INT4 path; the framework's linear-byte-scaling convention under-states d-Matrix INT4 / FP4 throughput by 2× as a known limitation. Fabric chain decomposes the in-server hierarchy into 4 tiers (cumulative reach 4 → 16 → 32 → 64): (1) `package_d2d` — 2×2 full mesh of 4 chiplets within a single Corsair package via D2D links, 128 GB/s, 0.115 μs (whitepaper Fig 6); (2) `dmx_bridge` — 4 packages joined across 2 cards via the DMX Bridge, 64 GB/s, 0.4 μs (whitepaper Fig 9); (3a) `pcie5-intra-hemisphere` — 2 sets of 2-card pairs (= 32 chiplets) within one PCIe-switch hemisphere, 64 GB/s, 0.6 μs; (3b) `pcie5-inter-hemisphere` — 2 hemispheres joined across the host-CPU PCIe switches (= 64 chiplets full server), 64 GB/s, 0.6 μs. Both PCIe hops use the same Gen5 x16 lanes; we model them as a single 2-tier `pcie_server` fabric with reach 2 × 2 = 4. Performance Mode tuners (placement=auto/auto) put weights+KV in SRAM when capacity allows; Capacity Mode pins weights to LPDDR5. Use this system for workloads that fit in a single 8-card server (e.g., Llama-3-8B per sram.md §3.2). For full-rack scale, use `dmatrix.squadrack` which adds the JetStream/Ethernet tier across servers.
